title: Grass on Denim
timeEstimate: 15–40 minutes; protein + chlorophyll combo, indigo dye-loss risk
stainChemistry: Grass is two stains in one: green chlorophyll pigment from the leaf plus plant protein the chlorophyll is bound to. The pigment is alcohol-soluble (rubbing alcohol pulls it out). The protein responds to cool water + Dawn. Hot water at any step sets the protein side and locks the green; once green sets, indigo denim is hard to recover without losing color too. Denim has its own complications: indigo sits on the surface of the fiber and any oxidizer (peroxide, chlorine bleach) will pull color along with the stain. Aggressive scrubbing on raw or dark denim leaves a halo ring around the treated spot. The recurring trap is reaching for hot water to 'lift' fresh grass — that's exactly what locks it.
whyThisWorks: Cool water plus Dawn handles the protein component without setting it. Isopropyl alcohol on a cloth pulls the green chlorophyll out — you can literally see it transfer. On denim specifically, the safer move is to favor alcohol over peroxide because alcohol leaves indigo alone while peroxide bleaches it. Air-dry is non-negotiable until the stain is gone — once the dryer hits residual chlorophyll, it sets permanently and adds polymer cross-linking from the protein side.
defaultAssumption: Kid soccer / yard work / lawn-tackle on dark or medium-wash denim. Fresh = high-confidence DIY. Hot-dried or sat-for-weeks = much harder; pro for dark indigo denim. Raw / selvage denim = pro from minute one (any moisture causes uneven fading).

spottingProtocol.instruction: Damp white cloth with COOL water. Press, lift, rotate. Cool only — heat sets the protein side. Never pour water directly on denim — causes water rings.
Drop of Dawn on damp cloth. Soft toothbrush. Always blot, never rub — denim ring-stains under aggressive scrubbing. Cool damp-cloth rinse.
Inside seam test: dab 91% IPA on a hidden seam, blot with white cloth. If indigo transfers, skip alcohol. If clean, alcohol is safe to use on the visible stain.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 91% IPA on white cotton cloth, ventilation
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply 91% IPA to a clean white cloth — never pour on denim. Tamp the green spot from the top, flip the cloth as it picks up green. Outside-in. 2–3 passes. Open windows; flammable, no flame.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean per label, COOL water, washing machine
spottingProtocol.instruction: OxiClean cool soak 30 min for the protein side. Then cool machine wash with normal detergent. NEVER hot water — heat sets residual chlorophyll plus protein.
AIR-DRY. Inspect in daylight. Faint green = repeat alcohol pass before the dryer touches it. Hot dryer on residual chlorophyll = permanent on denim plus indigo cross-linking.

steps: 3. Hidden-seam IPA test on indigo / dark denim.
4. 91% IPA cloth tamp for chlorophyll, outside-in, 2–3 passes.
5. Cool OxiClean soak 30 min plus cool machine wash.
6. Air-dry plus daylight inspect — never heat until clear.
products: 91% Isopropyl alcohol (hidden seam test on indigo first)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets protein and locks chlorophyll on denim
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets the protein side permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach — strips indigo unevenly, leaves halo
NEVER hot dryer until stain is fully gone
Hidden-seam IPA test on indigo / dark / raw denim
Always blot, never rub — denim rings under aggressive scrubbing
Never pour solvent or water directly — pour on cloth instead

homeSolutions.agent: Two Stains in One — Pigment Plus Protein
homeSolutions.instruction: Grass is the green pigment plus plant protein. Each part needs different treatment: cool Dawn for the protein side, rubbing alcohol for the green. Doing only one half is why grass stains come back after the first wash. Two hard rules on denim: NO hot water (sets the protein, locks the green) and NO chlorine bleach (strips indigo unevenly, leaves a halo).

Damp a white cloth with COOL water. Press the spot, lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. Cool only. Never pour water directly on denim — it causes water rings that are their own stain. Always blot, never rub — rubbing causes a halo on dark denim.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn First — Cut the Protein Side
homeSolutions.instruction: Drop of Dawn on a damp cloth. Work it into the spot with a soft toothbrush for 5 minutes. Damp-cloth rinse. This handles the protein part of the stain. The chlorophyll is still in there — the alcohol step pulls it out next.

homeSolutions.agent: Hidden-Seam Test on Dark or Raw Denim
homeSolutions.instruction: Before any alcohol on dark or raw denim: dab a drop of 91% isopropyl alcohol on a hidden inside seam, blot with a white cloth. If the cloth picks up indigo, your denim isn't colorfast — skip alcohol and go to the cool wash step. If the cloth comes up clean, alcohol is safe on the visible stain. Light-wash denim is usually fine; raw / dark / selvage often isn't.

homeSolutions.agent: Isopropyl Alcohol on a Cloth — Pulls Out the Green
homeSolutions.instruction: Apply 91% isopropyl alcohol to a clean white cloth — never pour on the denim. Press the green spot, flip the cloth as it picks up green, repeat. Outside-in. You can literally see the chlorophyll transfer. 2 to 3 passes is usually enough. Open windows — alcohol is flammable, no flame.

OxiClean per label in COOL water, soak 30 minutes. Then cool machine wash with normal detergent. NEVER hot water — heat at any step sets residual chlorophyll permanently. OxiClean is safer on indigo than chlorine bleach but still test hidden if your denim is raw or selvage.

homeSolutions.agent: Air-Dry and Inspect Before Any Dryer Heat
homeSolutions.instruction: Hang to AIR-DRY. Inspect in daylight before the dryer goes anywhere near it. Faint green left? Repeat the alcohol pass. Only put it in the dryer once the stain is invisible in natural light. Hot dryer on residual chlorophyll = permanent on denim.

steps: 1. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach.
2. Cool damp-cloth blot — never pour, never rub.
4. Hidden-seam IPA test on dark / raw denim before alcohol.
5. 91% IPA on cloth, tamp green spot outside-in, 2–3 passes.
6. OxiClean cool soak 30 min plus cool machine wash.
7. AIR-DRY plus daylight inspect — no dryer until fully clear.
products: 91% Isopropyl alcohol (hidden seam test on indigo)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent). Stop if hidden-seam test shows indigo loss — pro is the right call. Stop on raw / selvage denim from minute one. Stop after 2 home rounds without progress.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets protein, locks chlorophyll
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
NEVER hot dryer until stain is gone
Hidden-seam IPA test on indigo / dark / raw denim
Always blot, never rub — denim halos under scrubbing
Flammable solvent — no flame near alcohol

neverDo: Never hot water on grass + denim — sets protein, locks chlorophyll
Never chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo, leaves halo
Never hot dryer until stain is fully clear
Never rub denim under treatment — causes ring stain
Never pour solvent or water directly on denim — apply to cloth
fiberSensitivities: Indigo / dark / dyed denim: hidden-seam IPA test mandatory
Raw / selvage denim: pro from minute one — any moisture causes uneven fading
Light-wash denim: most forgiving — full home protocol applicable
Stretch denim with elastane: cool only — alcohol can stress elastane over time
Black overdyed denim: pro — color-loss risk on any oxidizer

materialWarnings: Indigo dye loss is the central denim risk — hidden test before alcohol
Raw / selvage denim = pro from minute one
No hot water / no hot dryer — heat sets both halves of the stain
Always blot, never rub — denim halos under scrubbing
No chlorine bleach on denim — uneven stripping

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent). Hidden-seam IPA test shows indigo loss. Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim. 2 home rounds plateau. Halo ring already forming around the spot.
escalation.whatToTell: "Grass on [light-wash / dark / indigo / raw / selvage] denim, age [fresh / days / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [Dawn cool + IPA cloth tamp + OxiClean cool soak + cool wash + air-dry]. No hot, no chlorine. [Residual green / halo ring / indigo loss / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with denim experience

contextQuestions: Light-wash, dark indigo, raw / selvage, or black-overdyed denim?
Fresh, days old, or hot-dried already?
Has chlorine bleach been used yet?
Mud component along with the grass?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Indigo dye loss is the recurring trap on denim. Hidden-seam IPA test is the safety check. Raw / selvage denim is pro from minute one because any moisture causes uneven fading. Black overdyed denim is similarly fragile. Mud + grass = brush mud off dry first, then run grass protocol. Hot-dried grass on denim is often permanent; managing expectations is part of the protocol.
